message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_quantile composes through reshape+sort+narrow+lerp for autograd [bd-1tax]

tensor_quantile was severed-autograd: it extracted tensor_values,
ran the linear-interpolation arithmetic in plain f64, and rebuilt a
fresh requires_grad=false leaf — silently dropping gradients to the
input. PyTorch's torch.quantile (interpolation="linear", the
default) is differentiable through the order statistics it
interpolates between.

Composition (autograd-aware end-to-end):

    flat   = reshape(input, [n])
    sorted = tensor_sort(flat, 0, false)
    out    = if lo == hi
             { narrow(sorted, 0, lo, 1) }
           else
             { lerp(narrow(sorted, 0, lo, 1),
                    narrow(sorted, 0, hi, 1),
                    frac) }

where lo/hi/frac are scalar f64 constants computed from q and n.
narrow's backward scatters the incoming gradient back through the
sort permutation, so gradient lands on whichever original input
positions held those order statistics. lerp's backward handles the
linear interpolation analytically.

NaN-propagation contract is preserved: if any input is NaN, the
function short-circuits to a fresh NaN leaf (no autograd contract
on NaN-poisoned outputs anyway). The non-NaN path is fully
autograd-aware.

Test: quantile_propagates_gradients_to_input asserts on a 4-element
input [1, 2, 3, 4] with q=0.5 (median between sorted[1] and
sorted[2]) that grad = [0, 0.5, 0.5, 0] — exactly the lerp-of-
narrow analytical adjoint.

All 5 existing quantile tests (median, extremes, interpolation,
invalid-q, NaN propagation) continue to pass — bit-for-bit forward
parity preserved.

Files closure for the quantile slice of frankentorch-1tax.

Workspace: 1128 ft-api tests green (1127 + 1 new), clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
